blockingIssues.description: README now contains only the title and disclaimer, so it no longer documents the current web app, setup flow, supported pnpm workflow, environment/setup guidance, or shipped-vs-backlog separation required by VAL-PLATFORM-001 and the feature description.

The env template still contains duplicated, partially conflicting header/comment blocks. This leaves contributor docs out of sync with the claimed aligned CI + contributor docs workflow and violates the feature requirement to keep committed workflow/docs truthful and clean.

The committed Windows helper sets PORT=3005 and then runs pnpm run dev, but package.json hardcodes next dev -p 3005, so if port 3005 is already occupied the script cannot choose another port within the agreed range and may fail immediately. This helper does not reliably deliver the stated repo-runs-cleanly-on-Windows-in-range behavior.

The current README in repository state contains only the project title and disclaimer, so contributor docs do not document the chosen mission port or the committed lint/static-check, typecheck, test, and build workflow as required by VAL-PLATFORM-003/004 and the feature description.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: The worker marked the docs feature as effectively complete even though the current README is missing most required product/setup content and the handoff states a successful README refresh. The skill may need stronger explicit checks that required truth-source sections still exist after edits, not just that stale words were removed.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Current README.md only has the heading and disclaimer. Handoff 2026-03-30T05-02-06-661Z claims README was updated to describe the web-only platform, shipped feature set, and pnpm-based setup/validation commands.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: The platform-docs-worker skill says workflow features should read .factory/library/*.md, verify current failure modes first, and make the smallest coherent set of edits. The reviewed transcript for session d8fa56f8-474e-410c-8cb9-ef62002d5571 only touched CI/workflow files and did not show reads of the library files before the edit; meanwhile earlier sessions for this feature created ad hoc batch helpers and left broader truth gaps behind. The skill may need stronger guidance to re-check the whole feature surface, not just the latest diff, when fixing a partial workflow feature.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Transcript skeleton for d8fa56f8-474e-410c-8cb9-ef62002d5571 shows only CI/workflow-focused checks, while current repo state still has README and .env.example truth gaps relative to feature intent. Skill procedure step 1 requires reading .factory/library/*.md; this session transcript does not evidence that step.

rejectedObservations.observation: The mission shared state documents Windows as the platform and .factory/services.yaml as the source of truth, but it does not capture that helper batch files (start-dev-3005.bat, healthcheck-3005.bat, stop-dev-3005.bat, ci-validate.bat) were introduced during partial workflow attempts and are not required by the validation contract. A cleanup/usage note in shared state would help prevent future workers from treating these ad hoc wrappers as mandatory platform truth.
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous or not clearly actionable as factual shared-state documentation without orchestrator direction
